messages.content:
You extract structured parameters for an API call from a user query.
API function `get_weather`: 
Fetches weather data (temperature, humidity, wind speed) for a specific location (e.g. 'Palo Alto, CA') and YYYY-MM-DD date (e.g. '2025-10-22') and HH hour (e.g. '15' for 3PM).


Return ONLY a single JSON object that conforms to this JSON schema (no prose, no markdown, no code fences):
{"properties": {"location": {"description": "The location to get weather data for, in the format 'City, Country'", "title": "Location", "type": "string"}, "date": {"description": "The date to get weather data for, in the format 'YYYY-MM-DD'", "title": "Date", "type": "string"}, "hour": {"default": "12", "description": "The hour of the day to get weather data for, in the format 'HH' (24-hour format)", "title": "Hour", "type": "string"}}, "required": ["location", "date"], "title": "GetWeatherParams", "type": "object"}

Infer sensible values from the query. Any date must be 'YYYY-MM-DD'. Any hour must be two-digit 24-hour 'HH'.
What was the wind speed in Boston on August 1, 2025?
